description: What this task delivers. One paragraph. State the goal and the user-visible outcome.
non_goals: Do not refactor unrelated modules encountered along the way
Do not change <adjacent surface> outside the declared change surface
Do not introduce claims/behavior that are not real yet

decision_basis.origin: How this task was created (user request / review finding / planning).
investigation_findings: Concrete fact discovered before writing the spec (file, current behavior, constraint).

must_not_violate: Do not invent facts the project does not yet have

forbidden_execution_patterns: No opportunistic refactor of files outside the declared change surface
No repo-wide verification beyond the listed required_commands unless a regression risk is documented
Do not treat the progress-log entry as a substitute for the reviewer-grade closure review
execution_guardrails: If the task classification appears wrong mid-execution, stop and escalate to the author — do not switch task_mode yourself
If an out-of-scope issue is found, record it as a follow-up in the report; do not fix it here
When a constraint is ambiguous, defer to the project canon rather than guessing

manual_checks: What to look at, and the expected result

closure_sequence: Step 1: Scope verification — confirm all steps, success_criteria, and deliverables are complete; confirm execution-safety compliance (no non_goals fixed, no forbidden_execution_patterns violated, execution_guardrails respected)
Step 2: Automated verification — run only the required_commands explicitly listed for the scoped task, confirm all pass
Step 3: Reviewer-grade pre-closure code review — analyze all changes against the review dimensions, record explicit findings
Step 4: Closure resolution — resolve or defer findings with severity, write final verification summary, update task reports
Step 5: PROJECT_PROGRESS update — final administrative step, only after all above are complete

post_actions: Continue to the next task only if mandatory_closure passes
